message:
chore(beads): close frankenpandas-fd90.25

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -455,7 +455,7 @@
 {"id":"frankenpandas-fd90.22","title":"test(readme): Parquet + Excel bytes round-trip coverage","description":"README §144-146 documents Parquet and Excel as round-trippable IO formats. Current coverage is one-way: fd90.290 calls .to_parquet_bytes() and .to_excel_bytes() via DataFrameIoExt but never reads them back. A regression in either format's parser would not be caught by the existing tests.

Plan: add readme_parquet_excel_round_trip exercising both via the standalone module-level fns:

- Build a DataFrame
- write_parquet_bytes(&df) → bytes; read_parquet_bytes(&bytes) → df'
- Assert shape + spot-check a Float64 value
- write_excel_bytes(&df) → bytes; read_excel_bytes(&bytes) → df'
- Assert shape + spot-check a value

Both pairs are in the prelude already. The test exercises them via prelude alone.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-27T12:27:47.806352161Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T12:29:33.714101401Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T12:29:33.712037402Z","close_reason":"completed: Parquet + Excel round-trip","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenpandas-fd90.22","depends_on_id":"frankenpandas-fd90","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-27T12:27:47.806352161Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-fd90.23","title":"test(readme): JSON / JSONL / Feather bytes round-trip coverage","description":"Sister to fd90.21 (IPC stream) / fd90.22 (Parquet + Excel). Three more documented IO formats have one-way write coverage but no integration round-trip:

- Feather: write_feather_bytes called in fd90.121 / fd90.288; read_feather / read_feather_bytes never invoked
- JSON: write_json_string in fd90.121; read_json_str never invoked
- JSONL: write_jsonl in readme_financial_data_pipeline; read_jsonl* never invoked

A regression in any parser would not surface. Plan: add readme_json_jsonl_feather_round_trip exercising all three:

- Build DataFrame
- For each format: write to bytes/string → read back → assert shape + spot-check value","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-27T12:30:22.359199734Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T12:32:15.499213491Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T12:32:15.496930206Z","close_reason":"completed: JSON/JSONL/Feather round-trip — all 8 documented IO formats now round-tripped","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenpandas-fd90.23","depends_on_id":"frankenpandas-fd90","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-27T12:30:22.359199734Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-fd90.24","title":"test(readme): non-default CsvReadOptions coverage (delimiter / no-header / na_values / usecols / nrows / skiprows)","description":"CsvReadOptions has 10+ pandas-parity fields (delimiter, has_headers, na_values, keep_default_na, na_filter, index_col, usecols, nrows, skiprows, dtype, parse_dates) but the existing readme_quick_example tests only use ::default(). A regression in any of these fields would not surface.

Plan: add readme_csv_options_round_trip exercising via prelude alone:

- Custom delimiter (semicolon-separated)
- has_headers=false (column names auto-generated as 0, 1, ...)
- na_values custom marker (e.g. 'MISSING' should round-trip to Null)
- usecols (column subset)
- nrows (truncated read)
- skiprows (skip preamble)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-27T12:33:55.745179646Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T12:36:33.088157501Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T12:36:33.086023457Z","close_reason":"completed: 6 CsvReadOptions fields covered","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenpandas-fd90.24","depends_on_id":"frankenpandas-fd90","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-27T12:33:55.745179646Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"frankenpandas-fd90.25","title":"test(readme): non-default CsvWriteOptions coverage (delimiter / na_rep / header / include_index / index_label)","description":"Sister to fd90.24 (CsvReadOptions). CsvWriteOptions has 5 pandas-parity fields (delimiter, na_rep, header, include_index, index_label). Both the struct and write_csv_string_with_options are in the prelude (since fd90.216) but have ZERO integration test coverage.

Plan: add readme_csv_write_options_round_trip exercising via prelude alone:
- delimiter = b';' (output uses ; separator)
- na_rep = 'NA' (Null cells render as 'NA' string, not empty)
- header = false (data only, no header line)
- include_index = true (index becomes first column)
- index_label = Some('row_id')","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-27T12:37:24.353156832Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T12:37:58.387254413Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenpandas-fd90.25","depends_on_id":"frankenpandas-fd90","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-27T12:37:24.353156832Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"frankenpandas-fd90.25","title":"test(readme): non-default CsvWriteOptions coverage (delimiter / na_rep / header / include_index / index_label)","description":"Sister to fd90.24 (CsvReadOptions). CsvWriteOptions has 5 pandas-parity fields (delimiter, na_rep, header, include_index, index_label). Both the struct and write_csv_string_with_options are in the prelude (since fd90.216) but have ZERO integration test coverage.

Plan: add readme_csv_write_options_round_trip exercising via prelude alone:
- delimiter = b';' (output uses ; separator)
- na_rep = 'NA' (Null cells render as 'NA' string, not empty)
- header = false (data only, no header line)
- include_index = true (index becomes first column)
- index_label = Some('row_id')","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-27T12:37:24.353156832Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T12:39:06.077982040Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T12:39:06.075842085Z","close_reason":"completed: 4 CsvWriteOptions fields covered","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenpandas-fd90.25","depends_on_id":"frankenpandas-fd90","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-27T12:37:24.353156832Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-fd90.3","title":"fp-io read_sql_table_columns chunksize helper","description":"Child slice of frankenpandas-fd90: add read_sql_table_columns chunk helper for pandas-style read_sql_table(..., columns=[...], chunksize=...) on the existing SqlConnection surface. Reuse table/column validation and projection query construction, return SqlChunkIterator, expose the umbrella re-export, and add focused fp-io tests for projection order, zero chunksize, and invalid projection inputs.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cod-pandas","created_at":"2026-04-25T07:51:48.478054749Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T07:58:32.073612091Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T07:58:32.071766828Z","close_reason":"Completed in 03a4ce7","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["io","parity","sql"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenpandas-fd90.3","depends_on_id":"frankenpandas-fd90","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-25T07:51:48.478054749Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-fd90.4","title":"fp-io SQL chunks index_col helpers","description":"Child slice of frankenpandas-fd90: add chunked SQL read helpers that combine pandas-style chunksize with index_col promotion. Cover read_sql/read_sql_query/read_sql_table chunk paths by wrapping SqlChunkIterator, preserving per-chunk RangeIndex when index_col is None and promoting/removing the named column when set. Add umbrella re-exports and focused fp-io tests for query chunks, table chunks, None behavior, and missing index_col errors.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cod-pandas","created_at":"2026-04-25T08:00:11.325655162Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T08:08:08.040272870Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T08:08:08.038384696Z","close_reason":"Completed in 90fd549","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["io","parity","sql"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenpandas-fd90.4","depends_on_id":"frankenpandas-fd90","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-25T08:00:11.325655162Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-fd90.5","title":"fp-io SQL options chunks index_col helpers","description":"Child slice of frankenpandas-fd90: add chunked SQL read helpers that combine SqlReadOptions with index_col promotion for read_sql/read_sql_query paths. Reuse read_sql_chunks_with_options plus the indexed chunk wrapper, expose umbrella re-exports, and add focused fp-io tests for params/parse_dates/coerce_float with index_col, None behavior, and missing index_col validation.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cod-pandas","created_at":"2026-04-25T08:09:51.139092537Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T08:16:06.447171200Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T08:16:06.445331175Z","close_reason":"Completed in 48c06e1","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["io","parity","sql"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenpandas-fd90.5","depends_on_id":"frankenpandas-fd90","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-25T08:09:51.139092537Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
